phase_1_database_restore.notes: PostgreSQL cluster restore. 156.2 GB at ~1.3 GB/min throughput.

phase_2_data_integrity.notes: SHA-256 checksum verification. 510 of 512 tables passed. 2 tables (audit_log_archive, session_cache) showed row count mismatch. Investigation: partitioned tables with partition pruning behavior difference between prod (PG 15.4) and DR (PG 15.3). Manual data verification confirmed data integrity intact. Root cause: DR PostgreSQL minor version mismatch causes different partition scan plans. Remediation: schedule DR PostgreSQL upgrade to 15.4 before Q4 test.

phase_3_rto_validation.notes: Total restore time 192 minutes against 240-minute RTO. 48-minute margin.

phase_4_application_validation.notes: API health checks: all 24 endpoints responding. Background job processing: 3 of 3 job queues active. User authentication flow: SSO login, MFA challenge, session creation all verified. Application functionality validation: PASS.

data_integrity_results.failure_root_cause: PostgreSQL minor version difference (15.4 vs 15.3) causes partition pruning differences. Data verified intact via manual inspection.
data_integrity_results.remediation: DR PostgreSQL upgrade to 15.4 scheduled for October 2025.

sign_off.comments: Test passed with 2 partition-related row count anomalies. Data integrity confirmed via manual verification. DR PostgreSQL upgrade scheduled.
